title: Getting started with roxyassert
text: R dynamically typed, function can handed wrong type hand back wrong shape complaint. roxyassert reads structured type annotations roxygen2 docs , document() time, generates per-function argument return assertion helpers — calls assert package — function’s documented contract runtime validation come one source. See README complete annotation grammar; vignette short tour plus pattern use .

title: Getting started with roxyassert
Getting started with roxyassert
text: type annotation parenthesised token start @param @return description. bare type vector; scalar declared explicitly. devtools::document() (re)writes R/contracts-generated.R assert_args_submit_order() assert_return_submit_order(), committed alongside NAMESPACE.
code: #' Submit an order. #' #' @param symbol (scalar<character>) normalised `BASE/QUOTE` pair. #' @param side (enum<character, "BUY" | "SELL">) order side. #' @param quantity (scalar<numeric>) order size. #' @param price_limit (scalar<numeric>?) limit price; `NULL` for market orders. #' @return (data.table) the accepted order: #' - order_id (character): exchange order id. #' - quantity (numeric): accepted size. #' - datetime (POSIXct): acceptance time. #' @export submit_order <- function(symbol, side, quantity, price_limit = NULL) {   assert_args_submit_order(symbol, side, quantity, price_limit)   # generated   result <- ...   return(assert_return_submit_order(result))                      # generated }

what: The pattern: an abstract class that enforces its own contract
title: Getting started with roxyassert
text: common use abstract base class whose public methods define uniform interface. Document method’s inputs outputs , let roxyassert generate checks — every concrete subclass inherits contract validated runtime, way way . method names scoped class (assert_args_AbstractStore__get), two classes can share method name without colliding. subclass overrides get() re-uses documented contract simply calling generated helpers.
code: #' @title AbstractStore #' @description A key-value store contract. Subclasses implement `.impl_*`; #'   the public methods validate inputs and returns from the documented types. AbstractStore <- R6::R6Class(   "AbstractStore",   public = list(     #' @description Fetch records by key.     #' @param keys (character) one or more keys to fetch.     #' @param limit (scalar<integer>?) optional max rows.     #' @return (data.table) the matched records:     #' - key (character): the record key.     #' - value (numeric): the stored value.     #' - updated_at (POSIXct): last-write time.     get = function(keys, limit = NULL) {       assert_args_AbstractStore__get(keys, limit)       # generated       result <- private$.impl_get(keys, limit)       return(assert_return_AbstractStore__get(result))  # generated     },      #' @description Write one record.     #' @param key (scalar<character>) the record key.     #' @param value (scalar<numeric>) the value to store.     #' @return (R6<AbstractStore>) self, invisibly (for chaining).     put = function(key, value) {       assert_args_AbstractStore__put(key, value)        # generated       private$.impl_put(key, value)       return(invisible(assert_return_AbstractStore__put(self)))     }   ),   private = list(     .impl_get = function(keys, limit) stop("not implemented"),     .impl_put = function(key, value) stop("not implemented")   ) )

what: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Write contract — roxygen2 docs — generate runtime checks. roxyassert turns structured type annotations roxygen2 documentation per-function assertion helpers. document() time parses @param/@return tags generates argument return checks — plain calls assert package — committed source file can read debug. R dynamically typed: nothing stops caller passing character expected number, function returning wrong shape. usual fix hand-write validation function — repeats, code, types already wrote docs, drifts . roxyassert makes documentation single source truth: annotations render humans generate checks, disagree.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: roxyassert roxygen2 roclet — runs documentation step (one writes NAMESPACE man/*.Rd), never R CMD build/check. parses tag text ; never executes code. assert depend roxyassert stays lightweight; roxyassert depends assert + roxygen2.
code: R/*.R  (your code + typed roxygen tags)      |      |   devtools::document()   <- roxyassert runs here, as a roclet      v   R/contracts-generated.R       <- generated assert_args_* / assert_return_* helpers      |      |   you call them inside your functions, and commit the file      v   runtime validation via the `assert` package

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: type annotation parenthesised token start @param @return description. Tags without leading (...) token ignored, adoption incremental opt-. Default arity follows R: bare type vector length. scalar (length 1) declared explicitly.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Every type valid wherever <type> appears (inline column/field bullet):

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: generic style explicit, uniform spelling: everything kind<type, ...> (shape C++ templates TypeScript’s Array<T>). ? modifier composes, e.g. (scalar<numeric>?).

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: list, data.table, data.frame describe contents markdown bullet list beneath tag. Bullets nest arbitrarily, can compose tables inside lists inside lists. bullet - name (type): description; bold around name optional (- **name** (type): ... also works). leaf checked atomic type, list-columns rejected construction — column declared (numeric) holds list fails.
code: #' @return (list) the query result: #' - ok (scalar<logical>): whether the query succeeded. #' - rows (data.table): matched rows: #'   - id (character): row identifier. #'   - value (numeric): the value. #' - meta (list): pagination metadata: #'   - page (scalar<integer>): current page. #'   - cursor (character?): next-page cursor, or NULL at the end.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
code: #' Submit an order. #' #' @param symbol (scalar<character>) normalised `BASE/QUOTE` pair. #' @param side (enum<character, "BUY" | "SELL">) order side. #' @param quantity (scalar<numeric>) order size in the base asset. #' @param price_limit (scalar<numeric>?) limit price; `NULL` for market orders. #' @return (data.table) the accepted order: #' - order_id (character): exchange order id. #' - status (character): order status. #' - quantity (numeric): accepted size. #' - datetime (POSIXct): acceptance time. #' @export submit_order <- function(symbol, side, quantity, price_limit = NULL) {   assert_args_submit_order(symbol, side, quantity, price_limit)   # generated   result <- ...   return(assert_return_submit_order(result))                      # generated }

what: Composed list with a nested table
title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
code: #' Run a screen and return results plus diagnostics. #' #' @param symbols (character) one or more `BASE/QUOTE` pairs. #' @param top_n (scalar<integer>) how many rows to keep. #' @param weights (vector<numeric, 1..>?) optional per-symbol weights. #' @return (list) the screen output: #' - matched (data.table): ranked matches: #'   - symbol (character): the pair. #'   - score (numeric): rank score. #'   - tags (character): label(s) on the match. #' - summary (list): run summary: #'   - n_in (scalar<integer>): symbols screened. #'   - n_out (scalar<integer>): rows returned. #'   - ran_at (scalar<POSIXct>): run timestamp. #' @export screen <- function(symbols, top_n, weights = NULL) {   assert_args_screen(symbols, top_n, weights)   result <- ...   return(assert_return_screen(result)) }

what: A data.table with mixed column types
title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: See Getting started vignette fuller pattern: abstract class whose every method enforces inputs outputs docs.
code: #' Open orders for a symbol. #' #' @param symbol (scalar<character>) the pair. #' @param statuses (enum<character, "OPEN" | "PARTIALLY_FILLED">) statuses to include. #' @return (data.table) the open orders, newest first: #' - order_id (character): exchange id. #' - side (factor): BUY or SELL. #' - quantity (numeric): remaining size. #' - reduce_only (logical): reduce-only flag. #' - created_at (POSIXct): submission time. #' @export open_orders <- function(symbol, statuses) {   assert_args_open_orders(symbol, statuses)   result <- ...   return(assert_return_open_orders(result)) }

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Two helpers, optional. assert_args_<fn> emitted function least one typed @param; assert_return_<fn> @return carries parseable type. Explicit arguments. assert_args_<fn>(arg1, arg2, ...) takes documented parameters name, declaration order, contract visible call site. Returns passed . assert_return_<fn>(value) validates returns value unchanged, can write return(assert_return_fn(x)) keep invisible(). invisible() irrelevant — generation depends typed @return, value returned. R6 methods scoped class. Method submit class Engine generates assert_args_Engine__submit (double-underscore separator), two classes sharing method name never collide; clashes abort generation rather overwrite. Internal, undocumented, committed. Generated helpers exported carry .Rd; live single R/contracts-generated.R (--edit banner) commit like NAMESPACE. package generates uses . Using optional. helpers generated regardless; calling opt-. can adopt one function, , — function typed tags simply left alone.

title: Generate Runtime Assertions from roxygen2 Documentation
text: Packages like typed declare types inside function body custom operator. works checks runtime, changes every function written. roxyassert keeps R code exactly treats documentation already write contract — checks generated , never write validation function hand , even choose call every one.
